objective: Review GitHub PR #115 (fix(upstream): suppress Accept-Encoding to fix OpenRouter SSE streaming batching), determine from the current source and upstream protocol behavior whether it is correct and release-safe, and land the best production-grade form into opencodex. Scope includes the PR diff, directly related upstream request/header construction, focused regression tests for SSE behavior and header preservation, CI/release documentation or devlog evidence required by this repository, and branch/release operations needed to promote the accepted change through dev, preview, and main. Preserve all unrelated user and agent changes, especially the untracked .claude/ tree; do not bundle unrelated refactors, dependency upgrades, or existing PRs/issues. DONE means an independently audited patch is merged or equivalently integrated, targeted and full relevant tests/typecheck/privacy/build gates pass, real activation evidence proves upstream requests no longer negotiate compressed/batched SSE where intended without breaking explicit user headers, dev/preview/main are pushed in the repository's established order, CI is green, and both npm preview and stable releases plus GitHub releases are verified. NOOP means the PR is already fully present or demonstrably unnecessary, with evidence. BLOCKED means credentials, branch protection, unavailable upstreams, or CI infrastructure prevent completion after reasonable retries. UNSAFE means the proposed transport behavior would create a material compatibility or security regression. NEEDS_HUMAN means only a maintainer policy judgment can resolve a conflict. BUDGET_EXHAUSTED applies only if the unattended wall-clock exceeds 90 minutes or release retries exceed three per stage. Use existing GitHub/npm credentials only, make no paid external calls, keep write scope to the PR-related implementation/tests/devlog plus release version commits, and verify with gh PR/check inspection, focused tests, bun test --isolate tests, bun run typecheck, bun run privacy:scan, bun run build:gui when package release gates require it, and npm/GitHub registry evidence. First work-phase: inspect and audit PR #115 against the current dev tree and write a diff-level implementation/merge plan before changing code.

workPhases.title: Audit PR #115 and freeze the integration plan

tasks.title: Inspect PR metadata, checks, review state, and exact diff against dev
Trace upstream header and SSE relay ownership in the current source
Write and independently audit a diff-level plan

workPhases.title: Integrate the accepted upstream transport fix

tasks.title: Merge, cherry-pick, or reproduce the audited minimal patch
Add or repair focused activation and compatibility tests
Run focused verification and adversarial review
Run full tests, typecheck, privacy scan, and package build gates
Commit or merge PR #115 and push dev
Wait for all required dev CI checks
Fast-forward preview and publish a unique preview version
Fast-forward main and publish the next stable version
Align dev/preview/main and verify npm, GitHub releases, provenance, and final CI

criteria.scenario: PR #115 is assessed against current dev and the SSE transport contract
criteria.expectedEvidence: PR diff/check metadata, path:line trace, diff-level devlog plan, independent audit verdict
criteria.capturedEvidence: PR #115 and issue #114 inspected; merge base bdc89503 and conflict-free merge-tree recorded; five diff-level plan docs written; reviewer 019f5989 returned blocking_issues: [] and VERDICT: PASS; WP1_CHECK_PASS observed Bun default gzip/deflate/br/zstd and identity override

criteria.scenario: The accepted patch suppresses upstream content encoding on the intended streaming path
criteria.expectedEvidence: Focused test or fault-injection assertion observing the outgoing Accept-Encoding behavior
criteria.capturedEvidence: tests/fetch-header-timeout.test.ts observed streaming identity on the wire and reproduced gzip-batched versus identity-incremental SSE; 10 local reruns and reviewer 20-rerun check passed

criteria.scenario: Header hardening preserves explicit user configuration and non-stream behavior where required
criteria.capturedEvidence: Record, tuple, and Headers explicit encodings won case-insensitively; non-stream retained Bun gzip negotiation; focused 48/0 and fresh reviewer blocking_issues=[] VERDICT PASS

criteria.scenario: The integrated tree passes production verification
criteria.expectedEvidence: Focused tests, full bun test, typecheck, privacy scan, GUI/package build output
criteria.capturedEvidence: Local production gate passed: bun test --isolate tests reported 2400 pass, 0 fail, 10047 expects across 229 files; typecheck, privacy scan, GUI/package build, and cached diff check exited zero

criteria.scenario: The change lands on dev with required CI green
criteria.expectedEvidence: Commit or merged PR SHA, origin/dev SHA, GitHub Actions URLs
criteria.capturedEvidence: Contributor SHA 75109049 preserved through merge 150873e6; hardening commit 54462c0f pushed to origin/dev; Cross-platform CI run 29223561042 succeeded across npm-global and Bun jobs on Windows, macOS, and Ubuntu

criteria.scenario: A preview package is published from the accepted tree
criteria.expectedEvidence: npm preview dist-tag/version, prerelease URL, CI and release workflow URLs
criteria.capturedEvidence: Published @bitkyc08/opencodex@2.7.11-preview.20260713 from fe302b79 via CI 29224218117 and OIDC release 29224361796; npm preview tag, SLSA v1 provenance, prerelease target, tarball/git SHA-256 equality e23d8df9..., and exact-SHA cadence test 3/0 verified

criteria.scenario: The stable release is published and all long-lived branches are aligned
criteria.expectedEvidence: npm latest version and provenance, GitHub stable release URL, equal remote branch SHAs, final CI success
criteria.capturedEvidence: Published @bitkyc08/opencodex@2.7.11 from e6d516ae via CI 29224504216 and OIDC release 29224642222; npm latest and SLSA v1 provenance verified; GitHub stable release targets e6d516ae; remote dev preview main all equal e6d516ae; final preview/dev CI and service runs 29224718979, 29224719112, 29224737093, 29224737095 all succeeded; PR #115 merged and issue #114 closed
